content:
<USER_REQUEST>
Create the following documentation and configuration files for an AI-powered passive income automation platform. All files go on D:\AI-Business\.

## FILE 1: D:\AI-Business\README.md
A comprehensive README for the entire platform. This is the MASTER document that any agent (AI or human) reads first to understand the entire system. Include:

1. **Project Overview**: An automated local platform that generates passive income products (KDP coloring books + Etsy wall art) using local AI models, orchestrated by a Python agent.
2. **Architecture Diagram** (ASCII): Show the flow: Orchestrator Agent → ComfyUI API → Image Generation → Post-Processing → PDF Assembly → Listing Generation → Output Package
3. **Directory Structure**: Full tree of D:\AI-Business\ and D:\ComfyUI\
4. **Hardware**: RTX 5070 12GB VRAM, 64GB RAM, Windows
5. **Software Stack**:
   - ComfyUI (image gen) at D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\
   - Ollama + Qwen 2.5 14B (writing)
   - Python scripts for orchestration
   - Specialized LoRAs: ColoringBook.Redmond for line art, base Flux for wall art
6. **How to Run**: Step-by-step for both humans and agents
7. **Troubleshooting**: Common issues and fixes (VRAM OOM, ComfyUI not responding, model loading errors, disk space)
8. **Agent Handoff Protocol**: How another AI agent can pick up where the current one left off. Include: what to read first, how to check system state, how to run diagnostics
9. **Maintenance Guide**: How to update models, add new niches, train new LoRAs

## FILE 2: D:\AI-Business\docs\AGENT_HANDOFF.md
Detailed handoff document for AI agents. Include:
- System state checklist (what to verify on startup)
- How to read logs
- How to run diagnostics
- Key file locations
- API endpoints (ComfyUI at 127.0.0.1:8188, Ollama at 127.0.0.1:11434)
- Error recovery procedures
- How to check if ComfyUI is running
- How to check disk space
- How to verify models are loaded
- Common failure modes and fixes

## FILE 3: D:\AI-Business\docs\ARCHITECTURE.md
Technical architecture document:
- Com
<truncated 547 bytes>
Can train custom LoRAs on RTX 5070 12GB using Kohya_ss with fused backward pass, block swapping, FP8 quantization. Training is slow but possible. 64GB RAM helps.
- Where each model lives on disk
- How to swap/update models
- Recommended specialized LoRAs from Civitai

## FILE 5: D:\AI-Business\skills\SKILL_comfyui_generation.md
A skills.md file that teaches an AI agent how to use ComfyUI API:
- How to start ComfyUI (the bat file or command)
- API endpoint: POST http://127.0.0.1:8188/prompt
- How to load and modify workflow JSON
- How to queue a prompt
- How to monitor via WebSocket
- How to retrieve generated images
- Example: generate a coloring page
- Example: generate wall art
- Error handling

## FILE 6: D:\AI-Business\skills\SKILL_pdf_assembly.md
Skills file for PDF assembly:
- How to use pdf_assembler.py
- CLI arguments
- KDP specifications (8.5x11, 300DPI, margins)
- How to verify output
- Common errors

## FILE 7: D:\AI-Business\skills\SKILL_listing_writer.md
Skills file for using Ollama to write product listings:
- Ollama API: POST http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate
- Model: qwen2.5:14b
- Templates for KDP descriptions
- Templates for Etsy listings (title, description, tags)
- How to generate SEO-optimized content
- Quality checklist

## FILE 8: D:\AI-Business\skills\SKILL_niche_research.md
Skills file for niche research:
- Manual research process (Amazon search, BSR check)
- How to validate a niche (BSR < 100K, < 200 reviews, $7.99+ price)
- Niche scoring criteria
- How to use the niche database
- Seasonal planning calendar

## FILE 9: D:\AI-Business\logs\README.md
Explains the logging system:
- Where logs are stored
- Log format
- How to read logs
- How to diagnose issues from logs
- Log rotation policy

Make all files detailed, well-formatted with markdown, and designed for both human readers and AI agents. Use clear headers, code blocks, tables, and step-by-step instructions throughout.
</USER_REQUEST>

123: - When you want a consistent "brand" style across all books
124: - When existing LoRAs don't match your target aesthetic exactly

127: 1. Collect 20-50 best coloring pages from your output
128: 2. Caption each image (or use auto-captioning)
129: 3. Configure Kohya_ss with 12GB presets
130: 4. Train for 1000-2000 steps
131: 5. Test the LoRA in ComfyUI

> **Last Updated**: 2026-06-14

| Model | Type | Size (Disk) | VRAM Usage | Location | Purpose |
|-------|------|-------------|------------|----------|---------|
| SDXL Base 1.0 | Checkpoint | ~7 GB | ~6 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\checkpoints\` | Coloring page generation (with LoRA) |
| Flux.1 Dev FP8 | Diffusion Model | ~17 GB | ~10 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\diffusion_models\` | Wall art generation |
| CLIP-L | Text Encoder | 235 MB | ~0.5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Text encoding for Flux |
| T5-XXL FP8 | Text Encoder | 4.9 GB | ~5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\text_encoders\` | Advanced text encoding for Flux |
| ColoringBook.Redmond | LoRA | ~200 MB | ~0.2 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\loras\` | Line art coloring book style |
| 4x-UltraSharp | Upscaler | 64 MB | ~0.5 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\upscale_models\` | 4× image upscaling for print |
| Flux VAE (ae.sft) | VAE | 320 MB | ~0.3 GB | `D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\vae\` | Latent-to-pixel decoding |
| Qwen 2.5 14B | LLM | ~9 GB | ~8 GB* | Managed by Ollama | Product listings & SEO copy |

> \* Ollama auto-offloads to system RAM when GPU VRAM is in use by ComfyUI.

---

| Property | Value |

| **Strengths** | Negative prompt support, huge LoRA ecosystem, fast generation (~8s/image) |
| **Weaknesses** | Lower quality than Flux for photorealism |

<truncated 19587 bytes>
----|-----|
+| `CUDA out of memory` | Model too large | Add `--lowvram` flag to ComfyUI |
+| OOM during upscaling | Image too large in VRAM | Upscale in tiles (ComfyUI has a tiled upscale node) |
+| OOM with Flux | Text encoders + model exceed 12GB | Ensure FP8 versions are used; add `--lowvram` |
+| Slow generation after OOM | Fragmented VRAM | Restart ComfyUI to clear VRAM |
+| Ollama very slow | GPU VRAM occupied by ComfyUI | Wait for ComfyUI to finish, or accept CPU-speed inference |
+
+---
+
+## Appendix A: Model Directory Map
+
+```
+D:\ComfyUI\ComfyUI\models\
+├── checkpoints\
+│   └── sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors          # SDXL for coloring pages
+├── diffusion_models\
+│   └── flux1-dev-fp8.safetensors           # Flux for wall art
+├── text_encoders\
+│   ├── clip_l.safetensors                  # CLIP-L for Flux
+│   └── t5xxl_fp8_e4m3fn.safetensors        # T5-XXL FP8 for Flux
+├── vae\
+│   └── ae.safetensors                      # Flux VAE
+├── loras\
+│   └── ColoringBookRedmond.safetensors     # Coloring book LoRA
+└── upscale_models\
+    └── 4x-UltraSharp.pth                  # 4× upscaler
+```
+
+## Appendix B: Quick Download Commands
+
+```bash

+ollama pull qwen2.5:14b
+
